title: Index corruption + catastrophic clobber on corrupt read
description: Concurrent/torn writes corrupted index.yaml; then upsertIndexEntry treated a corrupt read as empty (?? emptyIndex) and rewrote the index with just the new entry, orphaning all others (professor_mari: 1269 entry files vs 33 index rows). Fixes: (1) atomic writeYaml via temp+rename — no torn files, kill-safe; (2) route promotion/cleanup writes through serialized mutateIndex (no more unlocked writeIndex); (3) upsertIndexEntry refuses to overwrite a present-but-unreadable index; (4) scripts/repair-indexes.mjs rebuilds indexes from entry files.
close_reason: Atomic writes + full serialization + upsert clobber-guard + repair script. tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: First-run onboarding: one-button import, honest progress, payoff
description: Onboarding flow that takes over the panel until imported/skipped (localStorage onboarded flag). Intro: headline + one line ('Import your past chats so your characters can remember them') + one big button. Scan -> ready (shows chats/calls/tokens estimate) -> running: real progress bar with 'Analyzing chat N of M' + live ETA + Cancel (no mystery spinner). Done: payoff 'Your characters remembered N moments across M conversations' + 'Open a chat' button. Graceful degradation: per-chat failures are caught, skipped, counted, and reported at the end; cancel stops between chats. Reuses loadImportChats/importOneChat/estimate-beats.
close_reason: Onboarding takeover + 5-state flow (intro/estimating/ready/running/done) + ETA + cancel + graceful skip/report + payoff CTA. node --check ok.

title: 'Current' working cache: relevance+recency loader, recency-gated core
description: Reframe what gets loaded into the prompt as a 'Current' working cache, separate from the short/long/core retention tiers. Loader selectEntries no longer always-injects core; instead ranks ALL non-done entries by lexical relevance to the recent conversation -> recency -> proven value -> lane, and fills to budget. Relevance (stopword-filtered keyword containment of summary vs recent user+AI text, passed from process-turn) is the miss-path that resurfaces dormant memories when their topic returns, avoiding the ossification of a pure-recency cache. Core is never pruned (retention unchanged) so it always resurfaces by relevance. Eviction from Current is lossless. Eidetic mode = load all (treat everything as Current; testing only).
close_reason: selectEntries reworked to relevance+recency+value fill; core no longer auto-injected (recency-gated, still never pruned); recentText threaded from process-turn; eidetic loads all. tsc clean, 53 tests pass, relevance verified.

title: Imported beats not retrievable — no companion ledger entry
description: The loader builds the injected <memory> block from the entry index, never the beats store. Live-chat Tier 2 created a companion character_topics entry per beat (retrievable), but the story-import pipeline called encodeBeat only — so imported beats were analyzed but never recallable (the original 'beats not attaching to characters' problem). Fix: pipeline now writes a companion entry per beat via shared companionEntryFromBeat(); POST /api/beats-to-entries + scripts/backfill-beats-entries.ts backfill existing beats. Ran backfill: professor_mari now has 353 active beat-companions.
close_reason: Pipeline writes companion entries; backfill endpoint + script created and run. tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Multi-character story import (one pass, beats routed per character)
description: Import a story once and produce separate beats for multiple characters who interact. Endpoint accepts assignments[{characterId,characterName,names}]; attributes once (shared cached job keyed by union of names), then loops the pipeline per character filtering to that character's speaker names and saving beats under its identity key. Chunks are disjoint by speaker so each is analyzed once (no extra LLM cost); resume works per character. UI: 'Characters in this story' rows (character picker + per-char names, remembered), + add character. Response is per-character; UI shows the breakdown.
close_reason: Endpoint loops pipeline over assignments sharing one attribution; UI assignment rows with character picker + remembered names; per-character result. tsc clean, 53 tests pass, ext node --check ok.

title: Resumable story imports (checkpoint attribution + incremental beats)
description: A long import took ~22min; a cancel/crash lost everything because beats were only encoded in a final batch. Now: (1) attribution windows cached in a per-import job file keyed by hash(text+options); (2) beats have deterministic ids and are encoded incrementally as each chunk finishes; (3) the pipeline skips chunks whose beat already exists. Re-running the same file+options auto-resumes — skips attributed windows and analyzed chunks. Clear-beats also clears jobs. UI shows 'N resumed'.
close_reason: story-jobs.ts (window cache) + deterministic beat ids + incremental encode + skip-existing in pipeline + endpoint orchestration + clearJobs on clear-beats. tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Ledger UI: tabbed layout (Memory/Import/Settings) + preserve scroll
description: renderPanel wiped innerHTML and rebuilt all sections (entries first, controls last) every action -> endless scrolling to reach controls + jump-to-top on every click (e.g. Analyze). Split into Memory/Import/Settings tabs; preserve .me-panel-content scrollTop across re-renders (reset only on tab switch). Tabs auto-expand their sections on entry.
close_reason: Implemented together: tabbed ledger + scroll preservation + cancel + external/bigger-windows + remembered names. node --check ok, tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Automated capture tiers (1/2/3) bypass dedup — duplicates regenerate
description: Only the manual [remember:] and ingest-commands paths call isDuplicate. Tier 1 (digest.ts createEntry / snapshotSession every 30 min), Tier 2 (sentiment, api.ts ~555), and Tier 3 (ambient, api.ts ~591) all call writeEntry+upsertIndexEntry directly with no dedup. This continuously regenerates the exact duplicates runCleanup removes — Tier 3 ambient re-saves stable facts ('user grew up in Texas') every time the topic resurfaces. Fix: shared createEntryIfUnique(scope,scopeId,{lane,summary,content,...}) that reads the index, filters by lane, checks isDuplicate, writes only if unique. Route all three tiers through it.
close_reason: Added shared dedup.ts (createEntryIfUnique + isDuplicate); routed Tier 1 (digest/snapshot), Tier 2 (sentiment), Tier 3 (ambient) through it. api.ts now imports the single isDuplicate. tsc clean, 49 tests pass.

title: Conversational soft clock: temporal context inference
description:
Why: Characters have no concept of time of day or day of week. Memories feel decontextualized — there is no way to know if something happened in the morning after a hard week or late at night when someone could not sleep. Temporal context makes memories feel like real experiences not just facts.

What: Per-chat soft-clock.yaml state file tracking inferred timeOfDay, dayOfWeek, lastSessionEndedAt. Keyword signal detector (good morning → morning, just got home → evening, can't sleep → late night, happy friday → friday, etc). Morning-after logic: if previous session ended evening/night and new session opens with morning signal, infer a day has passed and update day-of-week. Inject current session context into memory block header so character knows roughly when they are. Enrich all encoded memories with timeContext field.
notes: No LLM needed — pure keyword matching. State stored per chatId. Feeds into all three capture tiers.
close_reason: Closed

title: Memory tier system: fields, loader, promotion/demotion engine
description:
Why: The current flat store treats a one-off mention the same as a deeply recurring theme. Memories need lifecycle management so important things persist and noise falls away naturally.

What: Add tier fields to Entry/IndexEntry (short|long|core|secondary_core), retrievalCount, recitationCount, cycleCount, lastRetrievedTurn. Update loader to always surface core/secondary_core before budget, short/long within budget. Add promotion engine (score >= 5 short→long, score >= 25 long→core) and demotion (not retrieved in 100 turns long→short, 50 turns short→pruned). Cycle counter: 3 promotion-demotion pairs → secondary_core regardless of tier.
notes: Score formula: retrievalCount + (recitationCount × 3). Demotion is time-based not score rollback. Core and secondary_core are never pruned.
close_reason: Closed

title: P3: Pending-speakers tab in ledger UI
description: New 'Pending speakers' tab (alongside memory/import/settings, see marinara-extender.js tab system ~line 919). Per row: speaker label + waiting-beat count + 'Suggested' badge w/ pre-selected character. Three actions: Create card (POST minimal card to Marinara /api/characters via marinara.apiFetch, then call resolve), Map to existing (character dropdown), Ignore (confirm). Import success state: 'Imported Z beats across N characters' + if pending>0 'Y beats waiting -> Resolve now' jumping to the tab. Depends on P2.
notes: Implemented (extension marinara-extender.js): Pending tab w/ live count badge, per-row label+count+suggested badge, Map dropdown (pre-selects suggestion by name), Create card (POST minimal Marinara card -> resolve create), Ignore (confirm). Resolve-now link in story-ingest result. loadPanelData refreshes count in background. node --check passes. NOT yet browser-verified — needs user to reinstall the extension in Marinara and test: import a story with an unknown speaker, confirm it appears in Pending, test map/create/ignore.

title: P2: speaker-resolution API endpoints
description: (a) GET pending speakers: unique labels, beat counts, fuzzy suggestion per label. (b) POST resolve: map-to-existing (add alias + migrate), create (caller supplies the new Marinara characterId -> add alias + migrate), ignore (move beats to ignored bucket). (c) alias CRUD (add/remove, dedup, collision detection). (d) cascade: on character delete, orphan its beats back to holding pool with original labels. Depends on P1.
close_reason: P2 shipped (commit pending push): /api/pending-speakers, /api/resolve-speaker (map|create|ignore), /api/aliases GET+POST(collision 409)+DELETE, /api/ignored-speakers + /api/restore-speaker, /api/orphan-character cascade. holding-pool PendingBeat supports analyzed re-home (no re-analysis). 69 tests. P3 (UI) unblocked.

title: P1: alias-table + holding-pool backbone, beat provenance, alias routing
description: Server-side foundation. (a) alias-table module in data dir: read/write data/aliases.yaml, map speaker label -> identityKey, exact match + jaro-winkler >=0.85 fuzzy, per-alias usage counts + addedAt, reserved 'user' canonical entry. (b) holding-pool module: data/holding-pool/ pendingBySpeaker, plus 30-day 'ignored' recoverable bucket. (c) add provenance to EmotionalBeat/BeatIndexEntry (sourceChatId, sourceMessageRange) at write time. (d) replace pipeline drop-path (pipeline.ts:84-95) with alias routing: exact->route, fuzzy->hold+suggested, miss->hold. (e) idempotent migratePendingBeats(speakerLabel, identityKey) reusing companionEntryFromBeat. Tests for match/route/migrate.
close_reason: P1 shipped (commits 13a28ff backbone + a5292a3 wiring): alias table (exact + jaro-winkler suggestions), holding pool (deferred-analysis, 30-day ignore bucket), beat sourceChatId provenance, idempotent migrate, orphan routing in /api/ingest-story (exact auto-route, fuzzy/collision/miss held, user skipped). 15 tests, 68 total. P2 unblocked.

title: Holding pool + alias table: orphan-beat routing & speaker resolution
description: Imports never lose beats to unknown speaker labels. A per-character alias table (keyed to identityKey) routes attributed beats; unmatched speakers land in a recoverable holding pool with fuzzy 'suggested' mappings; a Pending-speakers UI resolves them (map / create-real-Marinara-card / ignore). Migration is idempotent (deterministic beat IDs). See spec in session. Create-card = POST minimal card to Marinara /api/characters via extension, then map held beats to the returned characterId.

title: Honest retrieval signal: stamp lastRetrievedAt on use, not on load
description: Loader stamped lastRetrievedAt on every surfaced entry — conflating 'loaded into context' with 'actually used', which inflated the recency signal and the feedback loop (loaded -> looks retrieved -> stays). Fixed: loader stamps lastAccessed + retrievalCount only; recordRecitation now stamps lastRetrievedAt (+ lastAccessed) when the model demonstrably uses the entry (recitation). daysSinceRetrieval (demotion) and the Current-cache recency now reflect real use. Safe for retention: prune guard (retrievalCount>0) and core permanence unchanged; never-used entries fall back to lastAccessed so imports aren't pruned.
close_reason: loader stamps lastAccessed+retrievalCount only; recordRecitation stamps lastRetrievedAt. tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Soft-clock: weight explicit presence statements over session time; stop break-nagging
description: User repeatedly stated leaving/returning (3x), but the character nagged them to 'take a break' as if it were a continuous multi-hour marathon — patronizing and wrong. Soft-clock had no presence concept and only read the AI's message for time signals. Fix: detect explicit away signals (I'm leaving/brb/on mobile/at the park/gtg/ttyl…) from the USER's message; any later message implicitly returns them (counts the round trip via awayReturns). formatClockContext surfaces 'the user steps away and comes back on their own schedule — they manage their own time' (or 'has stepped away'). MEMORY_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS now tell the character to treat presence as fact and NEVER tell the user to rest/take a break based on session length. updateSoftClock takes the user's message (process-turn passes userMessageText).
close_reason: Presence tracking (away signals + implicit return) + context note + instruction against break-nagging + userMessageText wired. tsc clean, 53 tests pass. Live verification pending.

title: Stream within-chat import progress to the browser (NDJSON)
description: A long single chat sat on 'Analyzing chat N of M' for minutes with no browser feedback. analyze-beats now hijacks the response and streams NDJSON: per-chunk {type:progress,current,total} then {type:done|cancelled|error}. Pipeline gained onProgress(current,total) called alongside the console reporter. Extension importOneChat reads the stream (response.body.getReader), updates panelState.importChatProgress, and shows a within-chat sub-bar ('moment X of Y in this chat') in the onboarding running screen + an X/Y count on the Import-tab row. Manual CORS on the hijacked response.
close_reason: NDJSON streaming (hijack + onProgress + reader) and stripLoneSurrogates at storage choke points. tsc clean, 53 tests pass, ext node --check ok.

title: Strip lone UTF-16 surrogates from entries (invalid-high-surrogate)
description: Truncating entry text mid-emoji (120/140/600/700-char slices) can leave an unpaired surrogate, which is invalid Unicode and makes the LLM request body fail to encode ('invalid high surrogate in string') — can break generations when the entry is injected into the prompt. Added stripLoneSurrogates() applied in writeEntry (summary+content) and upsertIndexEntry (summary), the universal storage choke points. New entries are clean; existing ones get cleaned on re-import.
close_reason: NDJSON streaming (hijack + onProgress + reader) and stripLoneSurrogates at storage choke points. tsc clean, 53 tests pass, ext node --check ok.

title: Support long chats (1k+ messages) in granular import
description: Long chats were truncated to 200 most-recent messages. Lifted: fetchChatMessages cap 200->5000; Fastify bodyLimit raised to 64MB (1200 long messages exceed the default 1MB POST limit); the pipeline already processes all messages with no cap. Added analyze-beats cancellation (request-abort -> AbortController -> pipeline signal) so a long chat (100+ analysis calls) can be cancelled mid-run; extension importOneChat uses an AbortController that cancelOnboarding aborts; partial beats are saved (incremental encode) and resume continues. Fixed a clean-reimport bug: the pipeline skip branch now re-derives a chunk's companion ledger entry from its stored beat, so a re-import (which clears companions by sourceChatId) doesn't lose companions for already-analyzed chunks.
close_reason: cap 5000 + bodyLimit 64MB + analyze-beats abort + importOneChat AbortController + skip-branch companion recreation. tsc clean, 53 tests pass, ext node --check ok.

title: Import scope (this character / all characters) + per-chat chooser
description: Onboarding/import flow gains a scope toggle: 'This character' (open char's chats) or 'All characters' (every character's chats, each routed to its own identity). loadAllChatsForImport() lists all chats tagged with characterId/characterName (via /characters name map + getChatCharacterId); importOneChat uses the chat's own character. Added a 'Choose which chats' checklist (select-all/none, per-chat) alongside 'Import all'. Estimate/progress/cancel/graceful-skip all apply across scopes; ready screen reports character count for all-scope.
close_reason: scope toggle + loadAllChatsForImport + per-chat importOneChat character + chooser checklist + onboardScanAll/Chosen. node --check ok.

title: Dedup across imports: source-tag entries + clean re-import + richer-wins cleanup
description: Granular re-import could pile up beats next to a chat's prior entries, and shallow digest summaries could sit beside rich beats. Added sourceChatId to Entry/IndexEntry; pipeline tags companion entries; analyze-beats accepts chatId and removeEntriesBySourceChat() clears that chat's prior entries before re-analyzing (clean replace; beats idempotent by id). Cleanup dedup now keeps the RICHER entry (a structured [emotion] beat companion supersedes a shallow summary of the same moment) instead of just higher score. Note: legacy untagged digest entries can't be surgically targeted (indistinguishable from snapshots/remembers) — handled by richer-wins cleanup + the Current cache.
close_reason: sourceChatId tagging through pipeline/dedup; removeEntriesBySourceChat clean-replace in analyze-beats; richness() prefers beat companions in cleanup. tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Import cost controls: pre-flight estimate, confirm gate, backoff
description: Bulk granular import could fire many LLM calls blind. Added POST /api/estimate-beats (chunk+classify only, no analysis calls) returning exact analysis-call count + token estimate; 'Import all' now runs a free estimate pass, shows a confirm with the count/tokens, and reuses the fetched messages for the real run. Analyzer external calls go through fetchWithBackoff (429/503/529 + Retry-After, exponential backoff w/ jitter). Per-chat Import stays for one-at-a-time.
close_reason: estimate-beats endpoint + src/http.ts fetchWithBackoff in analyzer + UI estimate/confirm in importAllChats. tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Granular past-chat import (sentiment pipeline, not shallow digest)
description: Past-chat import called /api/digest (one LLM pass -> ~3-8 summary entries). Now routes through /api/analyze-beats -> full sentiment pipeline (chunk/classify/per-chunk analyze -> beats + retrievable companion entries), the same depth a live chat builds. analyze-beats takes a title for per-chat console progress. Per-beat dedup makes 'import all' re-runnable/idempotent. Import-all button already iterates.
close_reason: importOneChat -> analyze-beats with sourceType chat + title; produces beats + companion entries. tsc clean, 53 tests pass, ext node --check ok.

title: Memory retrieval observability — confirm characters are actually using memories
description: Need a clearer, ideally user-facing way to see whether a character retrieved/used memories on a turn, rather than inferring from sidecar logs. Existing signals today: (1) sidecar logs '[ME] memory loaded — key:.. chat:..' per memory-block write; (2) ME_DEBUG=1 -> [ME:loader] logs 'entries selected — chat:X char:Y global:Z' and the selected entry ids each turn; (3) recitation detection logs when a surfaced memory was echoed in the response and bumps recitationCount; (4) retrievalCount/recitationCount/lastRetrievedAt live on each index entry. Possible work: surface per-turn 'surfaced N memories (which ones)' in the ledger UI; show retrieval/recitation counts + lastRetrieved per entry in the panel; a small 'last turn' debug overlay. Deferred by user for later examination.

title: Character-names field in import, remembered per character
description: Import panel has a Names field (comma-separated) sent as the characters[] speaker filter, so 'Mari' pins 'Dr. Mari Zielińska'. Persisted in localStorage per characterId and auto-prefilled on character switch — type it once, remembered after.
close_reason: Implemented together: tabbed ledger + scroll preservation + cancel + external/bigger-windows + remembered names. node --check ok, tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Cancel/restart a running story import from the ledger
description: Import was one un-abortable await. Added Cancel button (AbortController), client aborts the fetch; server detects req close -> AbortController -> pipeline/parser check signal at window/chunk boundaries and stop before writing beats. Client resets immediately so a restart is possible.
close_reason: Implemented together: tabbed ledger + scroll preservation + cancel + external/bigger-windows + remembered names. node --check ok, tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: External-API attribution checkbox + bigger windows
description: Import panel checkbox routes attribution straight to external API and uses larger windows (28k vs 12k chars), fewer round-trips and more consistent speaker labels. Server: ParseStoryOptions.useExternal; attributeWindow skips local; external max_tokens raised to 16384.
close_reason: Implemented together: tabbed ledger + scroll preservation + cancel + external/bigger-windows + remembered names. node --check ok, tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Attribution phase shows no progress during multi-window story import
description: parseStoryToMessages attributes long inputs in N sequential LLM calls (up to 120s each on local) but emitted no progress after 'attributing in N windows' — looked hung. Added onWindow callback; ingest-story ticks a per-window bar (Progress.tick unit='window'). Server-side only.
close_reason: onWindow callback + per-window bar in ingest-story; tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Story-import progress reporting in sidecar console (toggleable, default on)
description: Live progress for story imports and chat-history analysis in the sidecar console: 'importing X — chunk i/N [bar] pct%', stage lines (attributing / parsing complete, analyzing sentiment), per-chunk ERROR with specific reason, and a done summary. New progress.ts (Progress class). analyzeChunks takes an onItem callback; pipeline threads ticks+errors. ingest-story accepts title (file name) and progress override; extension sends the file name. Toggle MARINARA_EXTENDER_PROGRESS (default on; 0 to silence), shown at startup.
close_reason: progress.ts + analyzer onItem + pipeline ticks + ingest-story title/progress + extension sends file name + env toggle (default on). Output verified; tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Drop Marinara embeddings sidecar; route chunk embeddings through Ollama
description: The only remaining Marinara Engine sidecar dependency was the chunker's embeddings call (MARINARA_ENGINE_URL/api/sidecar/v1/embeddings) for semantic merging. Replaced with Ollama's /v1/embeddings, opt-in via MARINARA_EXTENDER_EMBED_MODEL; unset = speaker-turn grouping (no external sidecar, no warning spam). All other model calls already used Ollama; corrected stale 'Gemma/Marinara sidecar' comments and error messages in digest/analyzer/pipeline/classifier. .env: removed unused MARINARA_ENGINE_URL, documented opt-in embed model.
close_reason: chunker uses Ollama /v1/embeddings (opt-in EMBED_MODEL); stale comments/errors fixed; tsc clean, 53 tests pass.

title: Pre-attribution false-positives on prose with stray colons
description: isPreAttributed counted any 'Capitalized: text' line, so date headers (Date:/Chat:), literal Narrator:, and prose sentences with a mid-line colon ('The one that meant ...:') hit the >=3 threshold. Story got treated as a dialogue log, skipped the LLM attribution pass, and the chunker split on garbage speakers (Chat, Date, The one that meant) -> 0-2 matching beats. Fix: require recurring speakers (count>=2) covering >=3 lines AND attributed lines >=40% of non-empty lines; blacklist header words; lazy speaker-label capture. Regression tests added.
close_reason: isPreAttributed now requires recurring non-blacklisted speakers covering >=3 lines AND >=40% attributed-line fraction; lazy label capture. 4 regression tests, all 53 pass.

title: Local model returns prose instead of JSON (ambient/digest/analyzer)
description: Local Ollama calls ask for JSON in the prompt but don't force it, so small models (dolphin3:8b) return prose and the code falls back to the external API every turn ([ME:ambient] local model returned prose). Fix: send response_format json on local calls. Ambient returns a bare array -> wrap schema as {facts:[...]} so json_object mode is valid; make parser accept both shapes. Apply to digest and analyzer local calls too.
close_reason: response_format json_object forced on local calls (ambient/digest/analyzer). Ambient schema wrapped as {facts:[...]}; parser accepts both shapes. Cuts the prose-fallback.

title: Analyzer context uses filtered neighbors, not true adjacent chunks
description: analyzeChunks sets before/after to passing[i-1]/passing[i+1] (nearest threshold-passing, speaker-filtered chunks), but the documented intent is the actual surrounding conversation. Distant cherry-picked beats are shown as 'preceding/following context for tone', degrading every analysis. Fix: thread the full ordered classification list through and pick true adjacent chunks by position. Same in api.ts Tier 2 caller.
close_reason: analyzeChunks now takes the full ordered classification list and picks true adjacent chunks by index for before/after context. pipeline and Tier 2 both pass the full list.

title: memory-block resolves identity without characterName -> cryptic slug
description: GET /api/memory-block calls resolveIdentity(characterId) with no name. It fires on session load (syncMemoryBlock), before the first process-turn. For a brand-new character this creates the stable identityKey from slugify(cardId) = the cryptic card ID instead of the character's name. process-turn later fixes the display name but the directory/key stays cryptic, defeating the identity system. Fix: have the extension send characterName to memory-block and pass it through to resolveIdentity.
close_reason: memory-block accepts characterName; extension sends it. New characters get readable identity slug.

title: Bracket bookmark weight always defaults to 0.5
description: MEMORY_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS tells the model to emit [bookmark: ... weight=0.8 ...] (unquoted), but writer.ts parseBracketParam only matches quoted values (key="..."). So weight is always undefined -> falls back to 0.5 for every bracket bookmark. surfaceBookmarks rolls Math.random()<weight, so all bookmarks surface at 50% regardless of the model's 0.1-0.9 signal. Fix: make parseBracketParam accept unquoted numeric values (key=value up to comma/bracket) OR quote weight in the instructions. Verified with a parse test.
close_reason: parseBracketParam now accepts unquoted values (weight=0.8) and quoted values with commas. Verified weight parses to 0.8.

title: Recitation detection: increment recitationCount when memory is used
description:
Why: Retrieval (memory surfaced in context) is a weak signal. Recitation (AI actually used the memory in its response) is a strong signal for promotion. Without detecting recitation, the scoring system is half-blind.

What: process-turn response includes surfacedIds (list of entry IDs that were selected for context that turn). Extension checks the AI response text against each surfaced entry summary using Jaccard similarity. If similarity > 0.3, calls PATCH /api/entries/:id/recite to increment recitationCount. Server recalculates score and triggers promotion if threshold crossed.
notes: Implemented end-to-end. Server: loadContext now returns self-contained surfaced[] (id+summary+scope+scopeId) instead of bare surfacedIds; process-turn forwards it. recordRecitation rewritten to use new serialized mutateIndex() helper (was doing an unlocked read-modify-write that could clobber retrieval-count stamping = corrupt-YAML race). Extension: detectRecitations no longer depends on panelState (covered only chat scope before) — now works for all scopes from the self-contained payload, and compares each response against the PREVIOUS turn's surfaced set (cached in lastSurfaced[chatId]) to avoid a fresh entry self-matching the response it was extracted from. Metric changed from symmetric Jaccard (mathematically ~0 for short summary vs long response, never fired) to asymmetric stopword-filtered containment; verified: clear use=0.50, unrelated=0.00, partial=0.17, stopword-only=0.00 at threshold 0.30. PENDING: live browser verification after extension reinstall + sidecar restart.
close_reason: Implemented; server type-checks, extension syntax-checks, containment metric unit-verified. Live browser test pending reinstall+restart.

title: Tier 3: ambient detail classifier
description:
Why: Throwaway lines that reveal who someone IS get lost because they carry no emotional weight. 'Priya has a level 400 character', 'I grew up in Texas', 'I cried at the end of MGS3' — these make someone feel known rather than just remembered. This is where the magic lives.

What: After each turn, extract candidate sentences from both user and AI messages. Filter to: sentences under 120 chars, containing first-person markers (I/my/me/we/my), not ending in ?. For each candidate, run a fast binary LLM call: 'does this reveal a stable fact about identity, history, preferences, or relationships?' If yes, extract the fact and save as user_topics (user message) or character_topics (AI message). Fires async — does not block lorebook update.
notes: Small local model (dolphin3:8b) handles this well. Binary output keeps it fast. Candidate filtering is the key — reduces LLM calls dramatically. User messages → user_topics, AI messages → character_topics.
close_reason: Closed

title: Tier 2: sentiment capture on live chat turns
description:
Why: Emotional peaks in conversation (breakthroughs, fights, confessions, crying together) should be captured automatically without the AI needing to self-tag. The sentiment pipeline already works for story ingests — it needs to run on live turns too.

What: process-turn receives recent messages (last 20) from extension. Runs classifyChunks on them (CPU only, no LLM). If any chunk exceeds 0.65 salience threshold, fires analyzeChunk async (does not block lorebook update). Saves EmotionalBeat under character beats directory plus a companion character_topics ledger entry describing the moment. Works in both RP and conversation mode.
notes: Threshold starts at 0.65, tune from there. Classifier is pure keyword/regex so it adds no latency. LLM analyzer fires only when threshold crossed. Game mode excluded for now.
close_reason: Closed

title: Tier 1: time-based session snapshots (30 min)
description:
Why: Long collaborative sessions leave no memory trace when nothing trips an importance signal. A 2.5 hour paper editing session is meaningful context that the current self-tagging model misses entirely.

What: Extension tracks lastSnapshotTime per chatId in localStorage. On each new message, if 30+ minutes have elapsed, sends last 40 messages to new /api/snapshot endpoint. Server runs a single lightweight LLM call: summarize what was discussed, extract user facts and ongoing work, save as character-scope entries. Prompt is distinct from the chat-import digest — framed as ongoing session summary not historical review.
notes: Reuses existing digest infrastructure. 30 min is user-specified. Last 40 messages is a reasonable window — tune if needed.
close_reason: Closed

title: Three-tier ambient memory capture system
description:
Why: The current self-tagging model only captures what the AI explicitly decides to flag — it misses two entire categories of valuable memory. A 2.5-hour paper editing session left no trace because nothing tripped an importance signal. Low-signal throwaway details ('Priya's level 400 character', 'the MGS3 tears') are what make someone feel *known* rather than just remembered, and no current signal catches them.

What needs to be done:

Tier 1 — Temporal snapshots: Periodic memory saves on a cron/timer, independent of emotional weight. Captures long collaborative sessions where content matters but nothing spikes. Simplest to implement — scheduled digest of recent turns into character memory.

Tier 2 — Sentiment-triggered: Emotional peaks flagged and stored automatically. Crying together, breakthroughs, fights, confessions. The sentiment pipeline (analyzer.ts) already scores salience — this tier is about wiring those scores into the memory write path for chat turns, not just story ingests.

Tier 3 — Ambient detail capture: The hard one. Throwaway lines that reveal who someone IS vs what they're doing right now. Needs a lightweight per-turn classifier that asks: 'does this sentence reveal a preference, history fact, or identity marker?' Low-signal, high-value. The difference between a therapist's notes and a best friend's memory. Possible approach: small prompt asking 'does this line reveal something about who this person is?' run against candidate sentences before they're discarded.
notes: User's exact framing: 'that third tier is where the magic lives — the difference between this AI remembers our conversations and this person actually knows me.'
close_reason: Closed

title: Global lorebook bleed: global-scope memories load for all characters
description: The global scope in the sidecar is intended for conventions that apply everywhere, but all global memories are injected for every character regardless of whether they're relevant. This pollutes context for characters that shouldn't see global entries, and there's no mechanism to restrict or opt characters out of specific global entries.
close_reason: Working as designed — global scope is intentionally injected for all characters. No actual bleed occurring.

title: Zombie entries: completed tasks leave empty entry shells in lorebook
description: When memory entries are marked done or deleted in the sidecar, the corresponding lorebook entry content goes empty but the entry shell remains in Marinara's lorebook. These zombie entries clutter the lorebook and waste context budget slots. Completed/deleted entries should be removed from the lorebook or their enabled flag set to false.
close_reason: Closed

title: Lorebook naming: all character lorebooks share identical name
description: Every character gets a lorebook named 'Marinara Extender Memory'. In Marinara's lorebook list this makes them indistinguishable — users can't tell which lorebook belongs to which character. Lorebook name should include the character name or ID so they're identifiable in the UI.
close_reason: Closed

title: Entry naming: no human-parseable scope/descriptor convention
description: Lorebook entries created by the extension have no readable name or consistent naming convention. The comment marker 'marinara-extender-memory-block' is an internal ID, not a label a user would understand. Entries should follow a scope/descriptor pattern so users know what they're looking at if they open the lorebook.
close_reason: Closed

title: P4: collision detection, auto-suggest aliases, group-chat verify
description: Polish/edge cases. Collision: same label maps to multiple characters -> detect at alias-add, user picks owner or marks ambiguous (per-beat manual). Auto-suggest: new chat introduces 'Dr. Chandrasekaran' when 'Chandrasekaran' already maps to a char but the longer form doesn't -> prompt to add alias. Verify multi-character group-chat routing still routes each beat individually. Depends on P1.

title: Configurable Current-cache token budgets (env now; per-character/per-model later)
description: Budgets were hardcoded (chat 4000/char 2000/global 1000) — fine for 200k models, costly for tighter ones. Now read at call time from MARINARA_EXTENDER_BUDGET_CHAT/_CHARACTER/_GLOBAL (documented in .env). FOLLOW-UP: per-character and/or per-model budget overrides (store on identity/character config) so different characters or connected models can have different footprints.

title: Decide: should mere exposure (retrievalCount on load) still drive promotion?
description: computeScore = retrievalCount + recitationCount*3. retrievalCount still increments on every LOAD, so an entry that merely surfaces a lot can promote to core without ever being demonstrably used. lastRetrievedAt is now use-only (honest), but score still rewards exposure. Decision needed: de-weight or drop retrievalCount from computeScore (promotion = proven use), weighed against recitation detection's blind spot (it misses paraphrased use, so pure-recitation promotion could under-credit genuinely-useful-but-not-echoed entries). Deferred for a deliberate call.
close_reason: Resolved: exposure credit is now relevance-gated in loader.ts selectEntries — retrievalCount increments only when an entry is SUMMONED (relevanceScore > 0.1), not when it merely rides in on the recency fallback. So mere exposure no longer drives promotion; relevance-driven exposure does, which becomes a more honest proxy for use as the Current cache improves. computeScore (retrievalCount + recitationCount*3) kept as-is. See commit.

title: start.ps1: interactive console with restart-server command
description: After services are ready, replace 'press any key to close' with a command loop: R restarts the Memory Extender sidecar (kills the launched cmd/npm/node tree, falls back to killing the port-3001 owner, relaunches, polls health), Q quits leaving services running. Saves the manual restart-the-sidecar dance after code changes.
close_reason: Added Start/Stop/Restart-Sidecar helpers and a command loop (R restart, Q quit). Parses clean; non-ASCII confined to comment separators.

title: Long stories truncated during attribution (8192 token cap, single call)
description: story-parser sends the whole story in one LLM call capped at 8192 output tokens with no input chunking. Output is ~as long as input, so stories past ~20k chars lose their tail (climax/ending never attributed or analyzed). Fix: window the input at paragraph boundaries and attribute each window, concatenating results.
close_reason: story-parser windows input at paragraph boundaries (12k chars), attributes each window local->external->paragraph, concatenates. Normal-size stories unchanged (single window).

title: beat.emotion uses keyword classifier, not LLM emotions[0]
description: encoder.ts sets primary emotion from the classifier keyword match; the LLM's richer emotions[0] is ignored for the stored primary and the [emotion] ledger tag. Prefer analysis.emotions[0] when present, fall back to classifier primaryEmotion.
close_reason: Tier 2 ledger tag now uses analysis.emotions[0] (LLM primary) with classifier fallback; beat.emotion stays the enum lane for typing/panel.

title: Dual narrator casing: 'narrator' vs 'Narrator'
description: Chunker emits lowercase 'narrator' for *action* lines; story path produces capital 'Narrator'. povCharacter relabel only matches capital. Works today for story import but is a latent trap. Normalize to one label.
close_reason: Chunker emits capital 'Narrator'; tests + type comment updated. Single canonical label across chunker and story parser.

title: Bookmark file writes are unserialized (race / corrupt YAML)
description: upsertBookmark, processResponse's writeBookmarks, ingest-commands bookmark append, and the bookmark PATCH/DELETE handlers all do read-modify-write on bookmarks.yaml without serializedWrite (the lock that fixed the index race). Lower frequency than the index but same corruption class. Fix: add a serialized mutateBookmarks helper and route bookmark writes through it.
close_reason: Added serialized mutateBookmarks; routed processResponse, ingest append, and bookmark PATCH/DELETE through it. Removed dead upsertBookmark.

title: soft-clock previousSessionTimeOfDay never cleared -> stale '(morning after)'
description: updateSoftClock sets previousSessionTimeOfDay on a morning-after inference but never clears it. formatClockContext then appends '(morning after evening)' to the session-context line on every subsequent turn indefinitely. Fix: clear previousSessionTimeOfDay after it has been surfaced once (or only show it on the turn it changed).
close_reason: updateSoftClock now retires previousSessionTimeOfDay the turn after it surfaces; '(morning after)' shows once.

title: User-requested memory save: persist facts/preferences on command
description: Users should be able to ask the AI to 'remember this' mid-conversation and have the extender write it to the appropriate lorebook tier. Examples: 'remember I prefer dark themes', 'save this list', 'remember my character's backstory'. The AI would emit a structured tag or signal, the extension would intercept it, classify the scope (chat vs character vs global), and call POST /api/entries to persist it as a named entry. Scope selection could be heuristic or user-directed via phrasing.
notes: Implemented via prompt directive (user-chosen approach over extension interception). The [remember:] command pipeline already handled extraction, dedup, scope routing, and tiering end-to-end; the only gap was that explicit user requests weren't a guaranteed trigger — the instructions discouraged trivial saves, so 'remember I prefer dark themes' would be dropped as minor. Added a 'WHEN THE USER ASKS YOU TO REMEMBER' block to MEMORY_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTIONS making a direct request a HARD trigger that overrides the discretionary rule, with lane/scope guidance (fact→user_topics, task→open_threads; character scope unless user says this-chat-only), a confirmation nudge, and a guard distinguishing real requests from incidental phrasing ('remember when we…'). Verified extractRememberTags parses the emitted bracket format incl. spaced content values. PENDING: live browser verification after sidecar restart.
close_reason: Implemented as prompt directive over the existing [remember:] pipeline; type-checks clean, parser path verified. Live browser test pending sidecar restart.

title: Inline Create-card produces a blank card — optionally flesh out via Marinara character-maker
description: Observed during P3 testing. The 'Create card' affordance (Pending tab + '+ New character' import dropdown) creates a minimal Marinara card { name, description:'' } so every other field is blank. Intended (we only need a characterId to route beats), but rough. Enhancement: offer to flesh the new card out via Marinara's AI character-maker (POST /api/character-maker, SSE) — e.g. an optional 'Generate details' step after creation, or pass a seed/brief. Keep the bare-create as the default/fast path. Out of scope for the holding-pool epic; backlog.

title: v1.1: IDF-weighted relevance scoring (retire stopword list)
description:
Post-MVP tweak — defer until the holding-pool epic and core features are working. Replace the equal-weight lexical containment in loader.ts relevanceScore with IDF-weighted containment so distinctive tokens (Emma, cover-letter, Hargrove) drive relevance instead of being drowned out by formulaic beat-summary vocabulary (feel/trust/wants/anxious). Subsumes and retires the hand-maintained RELEVANCE_STOPWORDS list.

DESIGN NOTES (from review):
- It's IDF, not TF-IDF: summaries are ~15 words so TF~=1 and does nothing. Score = sum(idf of matched summary tokens) / sum(idf of all summary tokens), preserving the 0-1 range and the directional summary->recentText property.
- Corpus = PER CHARACTER, not per-scope. A fresh chat has too few entries for stable IDF; token rarity is a property of the character's domain. Build one idf table per character, reuse across that character's chat/character/global-relevant scopes.
- Smoothing: df+1 for unseen tokens so a brand-new distinctive token gets ~max weight (correct) rather than divide-by-zero.
- Build cost is trivial (one pass, ~18k tokens for a 900-entry character). Rebuild lazily on the promotion pass or every N saves; cache in memory keyed by character. Per-turn lookup stays O(summary tokens) — zero added latency, no model call.
- RECALIBRATE the RELEVANCE_CREDIT_THRESHOLD (currently 0.1 in loader.ts): IDF weighting shifts the score distribution, so the 'summoned' gate that drives retrievalCount/exposure-credit changes meaning. Re-tune against real data, don't assume 0.1 holds.
- Alternative considered: embeddings cosine (reuses Ollama EMBED_MODEL) — rejected for v1.1 because it trades the zero-latency/zero-cost property for a per-turn model call. IDF keeps the cheap-lookup property.

title: Beat index writes unserialized; config path breaks on spaces
description: upsertBeatIndex does unlocked read-modify-write (race class). config.ts CONFIG_DIR uses import.meta.url pathname which percent-encodes spaces; use fileURLToPath.
close_reason: upsertBeatIndex serialized per character; config.ts uses fileURLToPath (handles spaces/drive letters).

value: Marinara Engine GET /api/chats/:id/messages returns ALL messages by default (storage.listMessages = SELECT ... WHERE chatId ORDER BY createdAt,id with no LIMIT). Pagination is opt-in via ?limit=N&before=cursor (listMessagesPaginated). Our extension fetchChatMessages sends no limit, so it gets the full history; the only client cap is .slice(-5000). Confirmed against Pasta-Devs/Marinara-Engine main, routes/chats.routes.ts + services/storage/chats.storage.ts.
